title: crepidomanes griffithii
intro: Crepidomanes griffithii, also known as Griffith's creeping fern, is a rare and endangered species of epiphytic fern native to the Western Ghats in India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Crepidomanes griffithii include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cognitive function, and provide anti-microbial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and powdered extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine and are believed to have therapeutic effects.
